messages.author: andrebruton
messages.text: 433.75MHz European Long/Fast George Repeater: AFBr (will change to ICAO code: GRJr)
messages.content_html: 433.75MHz European Long/Fast George Repeater: AFBr (will change to ICAO code: GRJr)

messages.author: andrebruton
messages.text: Repeater up and running on 433.75 GRJr
messages.content_html: Repeater up and running on 433.75 GRJr

messages.author: andrebruton
messages.text: Note: South Africa will use 433.75 EU Both PE and Garden Route are using it. Cape Town using 800's frequency range...
messages.content_html: Note: South Africa will use 433.75 EU Both PE and Garden Route are using it. Cape Town using 800's frequency range...

messages.author: Delta1
messages.text: Yes, Cape Town using 868MHz UK/EU Long Range specs
messages.content_html: Yes, Cape Town using 868MHz UK/EU Long Range specs
messages.reply_html:
andrebruton
Note: South Africa will use 433.75 EU Both PE and Garden Route are using it. Cape Town using 800's frequency range...

messages.author: Gary Mortimer
messages.content_html: Midlands/Durban using 868 UK/EU as well (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Delta1
messages.text: I think 868 MHz is the easiest band to use due to the availability of devices and the fact that many people that started out on Meshtastic have the 868 MHz devices. It is only a matter of time before more of them start testing Meshcore out as they realise that MT doesn't work very well with larger meshes.
messages.content_html: I think 868 MHz is the easiest band to use due to the availability of devices and the fact that many people that started out on Meshtastic have the 868 MHz devices. It is only a matter of time before more of them start testing Meshcore out as they realise that MT doesn't work very well with larger meshes.

messages.author: Gary Mortimer
messages.text: Fingers crossed, I will be deploying 4 more 868 repeaters in the Berg shortly (after Christmas) Hopefully they will see some data.
messages.content_html: Fingers crossed, I will be deploying 4 more 868 repeaters in the Berg shortly (after Christmas) Hopefully they will see some data.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Delta1
messages.text: I'm going to be in the Wilderness area in a couple of weeks. Will sniff around there with a Meshcore node as well.
messages.content_html: I'm going to be in the Wilderness area in a couple of weeks. Will sniff around there with a Meshcore node as well.
messages.reactions_html: 1
